title: Steam Integration
description: Steam-specific features: networking, workshop mod sharing, stats, user profiles, and achievements

actors.name: Player
actors.description: Steam user playing the game (client or host)

actors.name: Server Host
actors.description: Player who hosts a multiplayer session via Steam lobby

actors.name: Steam Platform
actors.description: Valve Steam backend: Matchmaking, Networking (P2P), UGC/Workshop, UserStats, Friends

use_cases.name: Host Multiplayer Server
use_cases.description: Creates a Steam lobby (Public or FriendsOnly), sets lobby metadata (name, map, wave, version, gamemode, bans), and starts the underlying NetProvider server.

use_cases.name: Join Server via Steam
use_cases.description: Player joins a Steam lobby using a steam: address or via friend invite. Verifies version compatibility, connects via P2P, and triggers client connection flow.

use_cases.name: Discover Steam Servers
use_cases.description: Requests list of public/compatible Steam lobbies worldwide, filters by version and ban status, then falls back to LAN discovery.
filters: Player must not be in lobby ban list

use_cases.name: Send / Receive P2P Packets
use_cases.description: Serializes game packets to ByteBuffer and sends via SteamNetworking P2P. Server accepts new connections on first packet. Supports reliable and unreliable modes.
use_cases.notes: Packets >= 1000 bytes are always sent reliably. StreamChunk uses ReliableWithBuffering.

use_cases.name: Invite Friends to Game
use_cases.description: Opens the Steam overlay invite dialog for the current lobby.

use_cases.name: Update Lobby Metadata
use_cases.description: Updates lobby data fields (wave, map name, gamemode) on wave events or new game. Also updates ban list when players are banned/unbanned.

use_cases.name: Publish Content to Workshop
use_cases.description: Publishes a Publishable item (Map, Schematic, LoadedMod) to Steam Workshop. Creates new item or redirects to existing. Requires EULA acceptance, then calls ugc.createItem() and update pipeline.

use_cases.name: Update Workshop Item
use_cases.description: Updates title, description, tags, preview image, content folder of an existing Workshop item. Shows progress bar during upload. On success opens Steam overlay to item page.

use_cases.name: View Workshop Listing
use_cases.description: Fetches UGC details for a published item. If player owns item, shows update/view dialog. Otherwise opens Steam overlay to community page.

use_cases.name: Browse & Download Workshop Content
use_cases.description: On startup, loads all subscribed Workshop items, categorizes them (Maps, Schematics, Mods) by file structure, and makes them available via getWorkshopFiles(type).
categorization: LoadedMod: folder with mod.json or mod.hjson

use_cases.name: Track & Store Steam Stats
use_cases.description: Requests current Steam stats on init. Periodically stores stats every 4 minutes if updated. Triggers service.init() after stats are received.
use_cases.notes: Save interval: 4 minutes. service.init() called after stats received.

use_cases.name: Complete Achievements
use_cases.description: Achievements are completed via Achievement enum. Triggered by game events or Workshop actions.
achievement_triggers: Achievement.downloadMapWorkshop — on map Workshop subscribe or download
Achievement.publishMap — on publishing a Map to Workshop

use_cases.name: Authenticate Steam User
use_cases.description: Wraps SteamUser API. Provides user identity (SteamID) used across SNet (ban checks, connection addressing) and SWorkshop (ownership verification).

key_relationships: SVars is global registry holding references to SNet, SStats, SWorkshop, SUser (steamID=1127400)
SNet implements NetProvider — wraps ArcNetProvider for non-Steam fallback
SNet.SteamConnection extends NetConnection — represents individual Steam P2P connection
SWorkshop depends_on SVars.user (ownership check) and SVars.net.friends (overlay navigation)
SNet depends_on SVars.user (ban check in onLobbyMatchList)
SStats triggers service.init() after stats are received from Steam
important_notes: Lobby metadata keys: name, mapname, version, versionType, wave, gamemode, hidden, banned
Version gate: lobbies with non-matching Version.build are filtered from discovery
Ban list stored in lobby data as comma-separated Steam account IDs prefixed with 'steam:'
P2P channels: channel 0 used for all packets
Workshop content types: Map (.msav), Schematic (.msch), LoadedMod (mod.json/mod.hjson folder)
Fallback to ArcNetProvider (TCP/LAN) when not using Steam lobby
